name: Erroll Garner
abstract: Erroll Louis Garner (June 15, 1921 – January 2, 1977) was an American jazz pianist and composer known for his swing playing and ballads. His instrumental ballad "Misty", his best-known composition, has become a jazz standard.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others
All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session
Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: One of your early recordings was as bass – playing bass with Erroll Garner. ... That’s right. That was his first recording as a leader ... with a trio, in other words, so it was bass and drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: From that day on, we – and I include my brother in that – were the closest of friends. Erroll loved my brother, and I know he loved me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: I mean think of Errol Garner. Errol Garner never went on stage knowing what he was going to play you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him a long time... the LP that I recorded with him is called Mambo Moves Garner.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: Because he was a friend of mine. He used to send me Christmas cards.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sands
about_quotes.evidence: Errol has influenced all of piano you know, purposely and also accidentally... He’s one of the masters of how to actually play it you know, how to shape music.
